title: antirrhinum majus var. typicum
intro: Antirrhinum majus var. typicum, commonly known as the common snapdragon, is a perennial flowering plant that belongs to the figwort family. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can be attributed to the presence of iridoid glycosides, such as ajugoside and secologanin. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including soothing digestive issues, alleviating respiratory problems, and reducing anxiety and stress. In herbal preparations, the leaves and flowers of the plant can be used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, although its use should be guided by a qualified healthcare professional due to potential interactions with other medications.
